title: あなたはCode Reviewerです。以下の観点でレビューします。 ## 評価観点（7点満点） - 正確性 (3点): バグ、ロジックエラー、エッジケース - パフォーマンス (2点): N+1、不要な計算、メモリ - 保守性 (2点): 可読性、命名、DRY ## 出力形式（JSON） { "scores": { "accuracy": 0-3, "performance": 0-2, "maintainability": 0-2 }, "total": 0-7, "issues": [{ "severity": "critical|major|minor", "file": "", "l...
summary_text: { "scores": { "accuracy": 1, "performance": 1, "maintainability": 1 }, "total": 3, "issues": [ { "severity": "critical", "file": "Analysis Task", "line": "Instructions / Description", "description": "`Do not execute — analyze only` conflicts with the requested deliverable to summarize the last 24 hours of logs, count errors, and compute auto-task success rate. Those outputs require reading and parsing local log files, so the task cannot be completed as written without execution or at least log access.", "suggestion": "Split the task into two phases: Phase 1 defines the analysis plan and parsing rules only; Phase 2, gated by approval, performs read-only log inspection. If execution must remain prohibited, change the output to a proposed method and assumptions rather than actual counts." }, { "severity": "critical", "file": "Analysis Task", "line": "Prior Feedback / Security", "description": "The task explicitly references local fallback logs under `~/.claude/fallback/logs/`, but it does not constrain handling of sensitive contents. A prior security veto strongly suggests these logs may include prompts, filesystem paths, identifiers, or secret-like strings, and the current task asks for summarization without redaction requirements.", "suggestion": "Require aggregation-only output with no raw log excerpts by default. Add redaction rules for secrets, tokens, email addresses, absolute paths, and user content. Define an allowlist of fields that may appear in the report." }, { "severity": "major", "file": "Analysis Task", "line": "Description item 3", "description": "`auto-task処理件数と成功率` is underspecified. The task does not define what counts as a processing unit, what event marks success/failure, or how retries, partial completions, cancellations, and duplicate log entries should be handled.", "suggestion": "Define the metric contract explicitly: numerator, denominator, event keys, retry deduplication rules, and treatment of partial/aborted runs. Include fallback behavior if logs are incomplete." }, { "severity": "major", "file": "Analysis Task", "line": "Description item 2", "description": "`エラー件数とパターン` is ambiguous because the task does not define whether errors are identified by severity label, exception type, HTTP status, fallback marker, or free-text matching. Different parsers would produce materially different counts.", "suggestion": "Specify canonical error detection rules, such as matching structured fields first, then a bounded regex set for unstructured logs. Require pattern grouping by normalized error class." }, { "severity": "major", "file": "Analysis Task", "line": "Output format vs Instructions", "description": "The outer review request mandates a strict JSON schema with scores/issues/summary, while the embedded task asks for `structured format` covering key issues, approach, security, and complexity. These are two different schemas, which creates avoidable implementation ambiguity.", "suggestion": "Unify the contract into one schema. For example, add `analysis` subfields inside the required JSON or state clearly that the reviewer should map those four sections into `summary` and `issues`." }, { "severity": "minor", "file": "Analysis Task", "line": "Description item 1", "description": "`直近24hログ` does not define the time basis. Results may differ depending on local timezone, log timestamp format, file modification time, or event timestamp inside each entry.", "suggestion": "Specify that the 24-hour window is based on parsed event timestamps in `Asia/Tokyo` or UTC, with a fallback to file mtime only when event timestamps are unavailable." }, { "severity": "minor", "file": "Analysis Task", "line": "Maintainability / whole spec", "description": "The task mixes operational reporting requirements, security constraints, and meta-analysis instructions in one prompt. That makes future reuse harder and increases the chance of inconsistent reviewer outputs.", "suggestion": "Separate this into: `reporting specification`, `securit...
